page.content: In the evaluation of the overall impact of the intervention on health outcomes, the data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive analysis of the differences observed between the case and control groups. The table highlights several key metrics, including the Crude Odds Ratio (Crude OR) and the Adjusted Odds Ratio (AOR), which are essential for understanding the effectiveness of the intervention under study. The Crude OR offers an initial assessment of the association between the intervention and the outcome, revealing significant variations in health outcomes when comparing the case group to the control group. Subsequently, the AOR adjusts for potential confounding variables, providing a more nuanced understanding of the relationship.

page.content: The corresponding p-values (P) associated with both the Crude OR and AOR further indicate the statistical significance of these findings, emphasizing the robustness of the results. Overall, the data underscores the importance of the intervention in influencing health outcomes, as evidenced by the disparities in the case and control groups. This analysis serves as a foundation for further exploration and discussion regarding the implications of these findings in clinical practice and public health initiatives.
